rationale: Replaces raw httpx exception strings with human-readable error messages across all SAP-facing endpoints in the backend. Adds `_format_sap_error(exc)` helpers in v1_routes_devtools.py, v1_routes_security.py, and v1_routes_monitoring.py that classify exceptions via isinstance(httpx.TimeoutException) plus exc_str.lower() string-matching on 'timeout'/'timed out' and return the canonical 'SAP system did not respond within the timeout period…' message; secondary branches handle httpx.ConnectError, TABLE_WITHOUT_DATA, HTTP 403/404/401 with contextual hints (S_RFC authorization, SICF service activation, credentials). v1_routes_monitoring.py also adds `_sap_error_with_hints()` which extends the format helper with extra hint suffixes (RFC_READ_TABLE auth, ICF service /sap/bc/soap/rfc, SSO/302 Principal Propagation). ~15 exception handlers in monitoring (short_dumps, dump_detail, jobs, app_logs, system_logs, HANA metrics, CCDB, overview, transports, ping, SSE investigation) are updated to call these helpers instead of `str(exc)`. v1_routes_admin.py check_system_availability and app.py _run_availability_pings both replace `str(exc)[:500]` with the same timeout-detection branch. adt_client.py SOAP call timeout swallows the exc reference (since the message is now static). Exception causes are preserved via `from exc` everywhere they were before. Not error-swallowing: every handler still propagates a clear message and the original exception chain. Clean targeted UX bugfix.
